title: Today's AI Brief: Gemini 3.5 - Frontier Intelligence with Action
summary: Google DeepMind introduces Gemini 3.5, a model designed for complex workflows and agentic actions.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Gemini 3.5: frontier intelligence with action

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's AI Brief. I've been following the latest developments in AI, and I have to say, Google DeepMind's new model, Gemini 3.5, has caught my attention. It's built to help execute complex workflows, which is a big deal in the AI world. I also stumbled upon some other interesting news - researchers at Meta are exploring ways to use AI to help combat climate change, and a new AI-powered tool is being used to detect diseases more accurately. Let's dive into these stories.

According to the Google DeepMind blog, Gemini 3.5 is designed for 'frontier intelligence with action.' This means it's capable of handling agentic workflows, which are essentially complex tasks that require multiple steps and decisions. Think of it like a super-smart assistant that can not only answer questions but also take actions on your behalf. For instance, it could potentially help automate tasks in industries like finance or healthcare.

The Meta researchers are using AI to analyze satellite images and predict climate patterns. This could help us better understand and mitigate the effects of climate change. And, on the medical front, the new AI-powered tool is being used to detect diseases like cancer more accurately. It's amazing to see how AI is being applied to real-world problems like this.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
